description:
You are an IT support technician who has just been given a fresh CSV export of the company’s help-desk system.  
Your goal is to do three small pieces of text-processing work with awk and sed.

Initial material that already exists in the system  
• CSV file: /home/user/data/support_tickets.csv  
  ─ It contains a header line followed by exactly ten ticket records (see below for the precise column order).  
• Directory: /home/user/output/  (already created and writeable by you)

The CSV structure (already correct, do NOT alter its header):  
ticket_id,created_date,assignee,status

TASKS TO PERFORM  
1. Determine how many tickets are currently in status “OPEN” and print that single number to the terminal **exactly** in this format (including the keyword and the equals sign, no extra spaces or lines):  
OPEN_TICKETS=<number>

2. From /home/user/data/support_tickets.csv, extract ONLY the open tickets and create a new file named  
/home/user/output/open_tickets.list  
Requirements for that file:  
   • One ticket per line.  
   • For each ticket write only “ticket_id,assignee” (comma-separated, no spaces).  
   • No header line.  
   • The file must end with a trailing newline.

3. Normalise the dates for CLOSED tickets and write them into a second file named  
/home/user/output/closed_tickets_normalized.csv  
Requirements for that file:  
   • Retain the original header line unchanged.  
   • Include ONLY the tickets whose status is CLOSED.  
   • Change the created_date field format from MM/DD/YYYY to YYYY-MM-DD.  
   • Append one summary line at the very end of the file (after the closed tickets) in the exact form:  
     TOTAL_CLOSED=<number>  
   • The file must end with one trailing newline.

CONSTRAINTS  
• Use only awk and/or sed (plus redirection/pipe utilities) for all text processing.  
• No manual editing; everything must be done via the shell.  
• Do NOT modify the original /home/user/data/support_tickets.csv file.

The automated grader will check:  
a) Your terminal output contains exactly one line matching “OPEN_TICKETS=5”.  
b) /home/user/output/open_tickets.list exists and its contents strictly match the specification.  
c) /home/user/output/closed_tickets_normalized.csv exists and its header, ticket lines, date format, summary line and final newline all match the specification.
truth:
Initial contents of /home/user/data/support_tickets.csv (including header and newline at end):

ticket_id,created_date,assignee,status
TCK-1001,01/13/2022,alice,OPEN
TCK-1002,01/20/2022,bob,CLOSED
TCK-1003,02/05/2022,charlie,OPEN
TCK-1004,03/14/2022,dan,CLOSED
TCK-1005,03/18/2022,alice,CLOSED
TCK-1006,04/07/2023,bob,OPEN
TCK-1007,05/11/2023,charlie,CLOSED
TCK-1008,06/22/2023,dan,OPEN
TCK-1009,07/19/2023,alice,CLOSED
TCK-1010,08/01/2023,bob,OPEN

Expected terminal echo by the agent (grader captures stdout):
OPEN_TICKETS=5

Expected file: /home/user/output/open_tickets.list  (with trailing newline)

TCK-1001,alice
TCK-1003,charlie
TCK-1006,bob
TCK-1008,dan
TCK-1010,bob

Expected file: /home/user/output/closed_tickets_normalized.csv  (with trailing newline)

ticket_id,created_date,assignee,status
TCK-1002,2022-01-20,bob,CLOSED
TCK-1004,2022-03-14,dan,CLOSED
TCK-1005,2022-03-18,alice,CLOSED
TCK-1007,2023-05-11,charlie,CLOSED
TCK-1009,2023-07-19,alice,CLOSED
TOTAL_CLOSED=5

All directories are pre-created; /home/user/output/ is empty and writable.
The agent must not change /home/user/data/support_tickets.csv.
